Class TransferFundsService

java.lang.Object
com.bank.core.application.service.TransferFundsService
All Implemented Interfaces:
TransferFundsUseCase
@Service public class TransferFundsService extends Object implements TransferFundsUseCase
Application service implementing the TransferFundsUseCase.

This is the most complex use case in the system. It orchestrates the following steps:

  1. Check Redis for a prior response to the supplied idempotencyKey; return the cached result immediately if found (idempotency guarantee).
  2. Load and lock both accounts from Postgres (pessimistic write lock).
  3. Validate account statuses and source balance.
  4. Apply debit/credit mutations on the domain entities.
  5. Persist both updated accounts and a new Transaction record.
  6. Atomically write an OutboxMessage to the outbox_messages table (Transactional Outbox Pattern — prevents dual-write).
  7. Store the idempotency response in Redis after the Postgres commit.

On any unrecoverable error, the Redis idempotency lock is released so the caller can safely retry.

Since:
1.0.0
Version:
1.0.0
Author:
Core Banking Team

  • Constructor Details

    • TransferFundsService

      public TransferFundsService(AccountRepository accountRepository, TransactionRepository transactionRepository, OutboxMessageRepository outboxMessageRepository, IdempotencyPort idempotencyPort, com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper objectMapper, io.micrometer.core.instrument.MeterRegistry meterRegistry)
      Constructs a TransferFundsService with all required dependencies.
      Parameters:
      accountRepository - account persistence port
      transactionRepository - transaction persistence port
      outboxMessageRepository - outbox persistence port
      idempotencyPort - Redis idempotency port
      objectMapper - Jackson serializer for payload and cache building
      meterRegistry - Micrometer registry for business metrics

  • Method Details

    • transfer

      @Transactional public Transaction transfer(TransferFundsUseCase.TransferCommand command)
      Executes a fund transfer from the source account to the destination account.

      If the idempotency key has been seen before within its TTL window, the cached Transaction result from the original request is returned immediately without re-processing.

      The entire operation runs within a single Postgres transaction. If anything fails after the idempotency lock is acquired but before the commit, the lock is released via IdempotencyPort.release(String) so that the caller can safely retry.

      Specified by:
      transfer in interface TransferFundsUseCase
      Parameters:
      command - the transfer command
      Returns:
      the completed or cached Transaction

    • validateAccountStatus

      private void validateAccountStatus(Account account)
      Validates that the given account is in AccountStatus.ACTIVE state.
      Parameters:
      account - the account to validate
      Throws:
      AccountBlockedException - if the account is blocked

    • buildTransactionPayload

      private String buildTransactionPayload(Transaction transaction)
      Serializes a Transaction to a JSON string for use as the outbox payload and the idempotency cache entry.
      Parameters:
      transaction - the transaction to serialize
      Returns:
      the JSON payload string

    • buildTransactionFromCache

      private Transaction buildTransactionFromCache(String cachedJson)
      Reconstructs a minimal Transaction domain object from a cached JSON string.

      This path is taken when the original Transaction row is not found in the database but a cached idempotency value is still present in Redis.

      Parameters:
      cachedJson - the JSON string stored in Redis
      Returns:
      a reconstituted Transaction
